title: Houses for Rent in Collinwood, Cleveland
metaTitle: Houses for Rent in Collinwood, Cleveland OH
metaDescription: Find houses for rent in Collinwood (44110), Cleveland. Rent ranges, RTA access, and Section 8-friendly homes from a local property manager. Book a showing.
answerBox: Collinwood, on Cleveland's far northeast side in ZIP code 44110, is one of the neighborhoods where we manage the most rental homes. Two- and three-bedroom houses and duplexes typically rent from about $750 to $1,300 a month, and every home we manage there accepts Housing Choice Vouchers.

body: Collinwood sits on Cleveland's far northeast side, largely within **ZIP code 44110**, split between the North Shore Collinwood lakefront area and the inland Collinwood-Nottingham section. It's one of the neighborhoods where our portfolio has its strongest presence, with more rental homes here than in almost any other Cleveland ZIP code we serve.

Housing here is a mix of early-1900s single-family homes and duplexes, many with front porches and small yards typical of Cleveland's older streetcar-era neighborhoods. Streets running north from St. Clair Avenue and Ivanhoe Road toward the lakefront tend to have larger, older homes, while blocks closer to Waterloo Road mix residential and small commercial buildings.

We manage voucher-friendly rental homes throughout Collinwood, part of our broader concentration of properties across Cleveland's northeast side. [See what's currently available](/p/book-showing) or [book a showing](/p/book-showing) to tell us your household size, budget, and move-in timeline.

sections.h2: How much is rent in Collinwood?
body: Two-bedroom houses in the 44110 area generally rent from about $750 to $1,000 a month, and three-bedroom homes commonly run from roughly $1,000 to $1,300, depending on condition, size, and whether utilities are included. Collinwood tends to price at or slightly below Cleveland's overall median asking rent, which Zumper's July 4, 2026 report put at **$1,250/mo** citywide.

RentCafe's July 2, 2026 report, which measures larger professionally-managed apartment buildings rather than individually owned rental houses, shows a citywide average of **$1,818** for a 2-bedroom apartment and **$2,716** for a 3-bedroom. Collinwood's older rental houses and duplexes generally cost noticeably less than those newer managed-apartment averages.

rows: Often with a basement and yard

body: Collinwood is served by RTA bus routes connecting to downtown and the Waterloo Arts District along Waterloo Road, one of the neighborhood's commercial and cultural corridors. The Red Line's Windermere station, the eastern terminus of the heavy-rail line connecting Cleveland Hopkins Airport to downtown and the east side, sits at the edge of the neighborhood.

Drivers can reach I-90 (Lakeland Freeway) within a few minutes from most of Collinwood, and downtown Cleveland is roughly a 15-minute drive southwest. The North Shore Collinwood section along the Lake Erie bluff offers additional green space beyond the neighborhood's residential streets.

items: **Transit:** Multiple RTA bus lines; near the Red Line's Windermere terminus
**Arts/commercial:** Waterloo Arts District along Waterloo Road
**Lakefront:** North Shore Collinwood section borders Lake Erie

body: Collinwood is one of our largest concentrations of Section 8-ready rental homes — every unit we manage there accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready. Keep in mind Ohio has no statewide source-of-income protection law, and the City of Cleveland does not currently require landlords to accept vouchers; only Cleveland Heights, South Euclid, University Heights, Warrensville Heights, and Linndale have local ordinances protecting voucher holders. Apply for a voucher through [CMHA](https://www.cmha.net), and see our [Cleveland Section 8 guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) for details on the process.

sections.h2: How do I apply for a house in Collinwood?
body: Applications for our Collinwood-area homes go through our online **DoorLoop** application portal, and every applicant goes through the same standard screening — we don't offer a way around income or background verification. What we can offer honestly is a willingness to work with vouchers, a range of income sources, and applicants who bring solid references or a co-signer if their credit is limited.

Start by [booking a showing](/p/book-showing) so you can see a specific home in person, then [apply online](/apply) once you're ready to move forward. You can also browse our broader [houses for rent in Cleveland](/houses-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) page if Collinwood isn't the only area you're considering.

faq.q: Are there Section 8 houses for rent in Collinwood?
faq.a: Yes. Collinwood (44110) is one of the neighborhoods where we manage the most rental homes, and every one of them accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready. Book a showing to see current availability.

faq.q: How much does it cost to rent a house in Collinwood?
faq.a: Two-bedroom homes generally rent from about $750 to $1,000 a month, and three-bedroom homes from roughly $1,000 to $1,300. Collinwood prices at or slightly below Cleveland's citywide median of $1,250 reported by Zumper in July 2026.

faq.q: What ZIP code is Collinwood in?
faq.a: Collinwood is primarily in ZIP code 44110 on Cleveland's far northeast side, spanning the North Shore Collinwood lakefront area and the inland Collinwood-Nottingham section.

faq.q: Is my Section 8 voucher accepted anywhere in Cleveland, including Collinwood?
faq.a: The City of Cleveland has no ordinance requiring landlords to accept vouchers, and Ohio has no statewide protection either — acceptance is up to each landlord. Every home we manage, including in Collinwood, welcomes vouchers regardless of what the law requires.

faq.q: Will I be approved for a Collinwood rental with a voucher or limited credit history?
faq.a: We don't guarantee approval for any applicant, and every application is screened the same way. We do welcome Housing Choice Vouchers and will consider references or a co-signer if your credit history is thin. Book a showing and submit an application to find out what fits your situation.
